nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
# PREPARATION (in Supabase)

- your database needs the extension 'pgvector' enabled -> select Database > Extension > Search for 'vector'
- make sure you have a table that has the following columns (if not, use the query below in the Supabase SQL Editor)

```
ALTER TABLE "YOUR TABLE NAME"
ADD COLUMN embedding VECTOR(1536), // check which number of dimensions you need (depends on the embed model)
ADD COLUMN metadata JSONB,
ADD COLUMN content TEXT;
```

- make sure you have the right policies set -> select Authentication > Policies
- make sure you have the custom function `match_documents` set up in Supabase -> This is needed for the Vector Store Node (as query name) 
(if not, use the query below in the Supabase SQL Editor to create that function)
- make sure you check the size of the AI model as it should be the same vector size for the table 
(e.g. OpenAI's Text-Embedding-3-Small uses 1536)

```
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.match_documents(
 filter JSONB,
 match_count INT,
 query_embedding VECTOR(1536) // should match same dimensions as from insertion
)
RETURNS TABLE (
 id BIGINT,
 content TEXT,
 metadata JSONB,
 embedding VECTOR(1536), // should match same dimensions as from insertion
 similarity FLOAT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $$
BEGIN
 RETURN QUERY
 SELECT
 v.id,
 v.content,
 v.metadata,
 v.embedding,
 1 - (v.embedding <=> match_documents.query_embedding) AS similarity
 FROM "YOUR TABLE NAME" v
 WHERE v.metadata @> filter
 ORDER BY v.embedding <=> match_documents.query_embedding
 LIMIT match_count;
END;
$$
;
```

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
# DELETION

At the moment n8n does not have a built-in Supabase Node to delete records in a Vector Database. For this you would typically use the HTTP Request node to make an authorized API call to Supabase. 

## HTTP Request Node

Use this node to send a DELETE request to your Supabase instance.

- Supabase API Endpoint: Use the appropriate URL for your Supabase project. The endpoint will typically look like this: [https://<your-supabase-ref>.supabase.co/rest/v1/<your-vector-table>]({{ $env.API_BASE_URL }} Replace `<your-supabase-ref>` and `<your-vector-table>` with your details.
### HEADERS:
- apikey: Your Supabase API key.
- Authorization: Bearer token with your Supabase JWT.
- Query Parameters: Use query parameters to specify which record(s) to delete. For example, `?id=eq.<your-record-id>` where `<your-record-id>` is the specific record ID you want to delete 
(You can also reference back to the **Retrieve Rows From Table** Node to get the ID dynamically)

Ensure you have the necessary permissions set up in Supabase to delete records through the API.

Please refer to the official n8n documentation for more detailed information on using the [HTTP Request Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

_Note:_ Deleting records is a sensitive operation, so make sure that your permissions are correctly configured and that you are targeting the correct records to avoid unwanted data loss.

parameters.content:
# INSERTING

- it's important to use the same embedding model when for any interaction with your vector database (inserting, upserting and retrieval)

# PREPARATION (in Supabase)

- your database needs the extension 'pgvector' enabled -> select Database > Extension > Search for 'vector'
- make sure you have a table that has the following columns (if not, use the query below in the Supabase SQL Editor)

```
ALTER TABLE "YOUR TABLE NAME"
ADD COLUMN embedding VECTOR(1536), // check which number of dimensions you need (depends on the embed model)
ADD COLUMN metadata JSONB,
ADD COLUMN content TEXT;
```

- make sure you have the right policies set -> select Authentication > Policies
- make sure you have the custom function `match_documents` set up in Supabase -> This is needed for the Vector Store Node (as query name) 
(if not, use the query below in the Supabase SQL Editor to create that function)
- make sure you check the size of the AI model as it should be the same vector size for the table 
(e.g. OpenAI's Text-Embedding-3-Small uses 1536)

```
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.match_documents(
 filter JSONB,
 match_count INT,
 query_embedding VECTOR(1536) // should match same dimensions as from insertion
)
RETURNS TABLE (
 id BIGINT,
 content TEXT,
 metadata JSONB,
 embedding VECTOR(1536), // should match same dimensions as from insertion
 similarity FLOAT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $$
BEGIN
 RETURN QUERY
 SELECT
 v.id,
 v.content,
 v.metadata,
 v.embedding,
 1 - (v.embedding <=> match_documents.query_embedding) AS similarity
 FROM "YOUR TABLE NAME" v
 WHERE v.metadata @> filter
 ORDER BY v.embedding <=> match_documents.query_embedding
 LIMIT match_count;
END;
$$
;
```

# DELETION

At the moment n8n does not have a built-in Supabase Node to delete records in a Vector Database. For this you would typically use the HTTP Request node to make an authorized API call to Supabase. 

## HTTP Request Node

Use this node to send a DELETE request to your Supabase instance.

- Supabase API Endpoint: Use the appropriate URL for your Supabase project. The endpoint will typically look like this: [https://<your-supabase-ref>.supabase.co/rest/v1/<your-vector-table>]({{ $env.API_BASE_URL }} Replace `<your-supabase-ref>` and `<your-vector-table>` with your details.
### HEADERS:
- apikey: Your Supabase API key.
- Authorization: Bearer token with your Supabase JWT.
- Query Parameters: Use query parameters to specify which record(s) to delete. For example, `?id=eq.<your-record-id>` where `<your-record-id>` is the specific record ID you want to delete 
(You can also reference back to the **Retrieve Rows From Table** Node to get the ID dynamically)

Ensure you have the necessary permissions set up in Supabase to delete records through the API.

Please refer to the official n8n documentation for more detailed information on using the [HTTP Request Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

_Note:_ Deleting records is a sensitive operation, so make sure that your permissions are correctly configured and that you are targeting the correct records to avoid unwanted data loss.

parameters.initialMessages:
=Hi there! 🙏

You can ask me anything about Venerable Geshe Kelsang Gyatso's Book - 'How To Transform Your Life'

What would you like to know?

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Manual Trigger".
Open the "Google Drive" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Default Data Loader" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Question and Answer Chain" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Vector Store Retriever" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Recursive Character Text Splitter1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "When chat message received" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Retrieve by Query" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings OpenAI Retrieval" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings OpenAI Insertion" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings OpenAI Upserting" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Insert Documents" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Retrieve Rows from Table" node (n8n-nodes-base.supabase) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Update Documents" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Customize Response".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Placeholder (File/Content to Upsert)".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
